items.myth: If Claude sounds confident, the answer must be correct.
items.reality: Fluency correlates poorly with truth; always verify critical claims.
items.example: A fabricated court case name can read authentically—search an official docket before citing.

items.myth: Claude can silently access my company's internal wiki.
items.reality: It only sees what you send in the session (plus enabled connectors/tools).
items.example: Asking 'our Q3 revenue' without data returns guesswork or refusal—not ERP lookup.
